messages.author: pelgraine
messages.text: I know nothing about what codec 2 encoded voice messages means but I love Meshcore and am very intruiged by the potential of this Mycelium client. I'd assume you'd need different hardware for it, maybe like the T3-S3 MSVR?

messages.author: Jude
messages.content_html: Looks really good (edited)
messages.reply_html:
Wattle
Hello! I’m building an open-source, extendable Meshcore client for my local community group. The project is still in an early stage, but the long term goal is to provide a flexible, modular client that adds richer functionality without disrupting the underlying mesh network or existing clients. Mycelium plans to support an extended protocol capable of handling larger chained packets in a controlled, realistic way. This includes features such as:
  • Sending small images
  • Codec 2 encoded voice messages
  • Rich messaging features (message edits, deletions, reactions, read receipts, etc.) All extended features will be designed with strict safeguards to avoid clogging or destabilizing the mesh. Larger messages will be limited (e.g. allowed only over zero hop, rate limited, or otherwise throttled) to ensure the mesh remains reliable for everyone. Once again this is in a very early stage! If you’d like to check out the project or contribute here are the links:
Mycelium client: https://github.com/WattleFoxxo/Mycelium Mycelium.js library and protocol: https://github.com/WattleFoxxo/Mycelium.js

messages.author: Jude
messages.text: On your phone are you just saving it as a web app? Wonder if you could use capacitor js and make it a full APK

It's a pwa (progressive web app) so it should work anywhere that has a web browser and it can easily be packaged into a apk
